items.title: He Blew the Whistle on DOGE. Then His Brakes Were Cut
items.summary: A federal IT staffer filed a complaint about DOGE, then went public. Shortly after Elon Musk boosted a post calling his claims false, his brake lines were cut. Now he’s suing for defamation.

items.title: Meet Microsoft Scout, Your AI Coworker That Never Logs Off
items.summary: Microsoft’s OpenClaw-style agent appears in Teams, just like a human colleague, and automates your dull office tasks.

items.title: Android Is Fighting Phone Scams With a New Feature to Prove Who’s Calling
items.summary: Available for Android 12 and later, the anti-scam feature is baked into Google Dialer, which sends a silent “confirmation signal” to ensure whoever’s calling you is who they appear to be.

items.title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses
items.summary: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million round led by Evolution Equity Partners.

items.title: Cyberdecks are having a moment, rejecting big tech surveillance with style and substance
items.summary: Over the last few months, these DIY hardware communities have exploded in popularity as people on social media show off their solar-powered game emulators, pocket-sized ereaders, and clamshell purse computers.

items.title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
items.summary: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon but partial truce with Hezbollah appears to hold
items.summary: Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US.

items.title: 'They'll fix the building, but not our souls': Sleepy Kyiv neighbourhood hit in Russian strike
items.summary: Scenes of devastation greet residents emerging from underground shelters after a major Russian attack.

items.title: British couple lose Iran jail sentence appeal, family says
items.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey.

items.title: Traders say Karen Bass and Spencer Pratt will advance to runoff in high-profile L.A. mayoral race
items.summary: Los Angeles voters go to the polls Tuesday. If no candidate receives more than 50%, the top-two vote getters will advance to a November runoff.

items.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon says markets are in 'greed' mode as AI companies seek billions
items.summary: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon's comments come as investors prepare for what will be one of the busiest periods for equity issuance in years.

items.title: Polymarket closes its first block trade as prediction markets push for Wall Street adoption
items.summary: Prediction market platforms have increasingly looked to institutional traders, not individual ones, as the next venue for growth.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Who is Bill Pulte, Trump’s pick for new intel chief
items.summary: President Trump on Tuesday named Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) head Bill Pulte to be the acting head of the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, replacing Tulsi Gabbard, who resigned last month. The move drew immediate fury from Democrats and pushback from Republicans, who questioned whether he had any qualifications for the role.…

items.title: Hostage situation, bomb threat reported at Chase Bank in California: Police
items.summary: Several nearby buildings have been placed on lockdown.

items.title: Pentagon hires convicted Jan. 6 rioter for counterterrorism post
items.summary: The Trump administration has hired a convicted Jan. 6, 2021, rioter to work in the Pentagon’s office that oversees sensitive special operations and irregular warfare matters. Elias Irizarry, who was 19 when he participated in the attack at the U.S. Capitol, was appointed to a position within the Defense Department’s Special Operations and Low Intensity Conflict…

items.title: Why SoundHound AI's CEO Says the Company Doesn't Need to Spend Excessively on AI Like Big Tech Giants
items.summary: Why SoundHound AI's CEO Says the Company Doesn't Need to Spend Excessively on AI Like Big Tech Giants

items.title: FedEx Freight embarks on journey as standalone LTL carrier
items.summary: FedEx Freight embarks on journey as standalone LTL carrier

items.title: Logistics M&A accelerates as WWEX and Auctane form ShipStation Global
items.summary: Logistics M&A accelerates as WWEX and Auctane form ShipStation Global

items.title: A hidden pollutant is changing how the world's forests breathe
items.summary: A massive global analysis found that nitrogen pollution can either speed up or dramatically slow the natural "breathing" of forest soils, depending on the ecosystem's condition. The results reveal hidden tipping points that could affect how forests store carbon and cope with climate change.

items.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
items.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

items.title: Scientists discover gut bacteria that may help protect against autism and ADHD
items.summary: A major study suggests that some of the groundwork for brain development may be shaped before birth through a surprising partnership between a baby’s genes and gut microbes. Researchers found that epigenetic changes present at birth can influence how the gut microbiome develops during the first year of life, and certain combinations were linked to early signs of autism and ADHD by age three.

items.title: Microsoft’s quantum chip got an upgrade. Critics are still skeptical
items.summary: Swapping materials in its Majorana 2 chip boosted the effectiveness of quantum bits that rely on the math of topology to reduce errors, Microsoft says.

items.title: The math of choosing a restaurant meal is revealed in Richard Feynman’s notes
items.summary: Physicist Richard Feynman turned a lunch dilemma into a math problem. Researchers finally cracked his notes and found people approximate his solution on their own.

items.title: More young people are looking to AI chatbots for mental health help
items.summary: A new survey estimates 8 million young people use AI chatbots for help when stressed, angry or sad, an increase from 2024.
